author: @bikerfirefarter7280
text:
Is it April?  Love you, but, No.
N-Rays, Luke.  No contact, no circuit, no current, no motor.
Look up 'N-Rays'.
If you get it right it doesn't need a 'little push' to start it.

replies.text: Funny that , I also checked the date .

author: @Charlie-Oooooo
text: Luke, nice presentation.  👍 But IMHO maybe you should have Rob do a quick technical review of the video, as I think you might have left out some important details.  I couldn't find an email address for you guys, otherwise I would have contacted you that way with the specifics.  Best Regards.

author: @GrandadIsAnOldMan
text: Luke, I have to ask. 03:33 Where is the conductor touching the magnet at the bottom to complete the circuit? It is not clear in the video. A close up of the base might help.

replies.author: @stewartpalmer2456
replies.text: I am with you here, I didn't see the contact on the bottom either. So how would the battery even discharge? Or is this strictly magnetic coupling?

replies.author: @GrandadIsAnOldMan
replies.text: @stewartpalmer2456 I don't want to say it, but I think Luke made a mistake. Or the camera angle is wrong. I have done plenty of these. He started right, but the final two versions don't appear to make contact. They need current to flow through the conductor to generate a magnet field. It will work with an intermittent contact, so it only needs to brush the magnet. It works best with a good continuous contact. So as I say, it may just be the camera angle, and it does make occasionally contact.
